title: Heat
boss_intro:
M.'s plane landed at six. She hasn't called. Carol is looking at the metrics table — different shape than logs. Pre-aggregated. Every minute, every service: n is total events, errors is error events. No raw messages. No tags column. Just counts.

Rate is what you get when you divide a count by time. Rate is what M. asked for.
arc_hook: Something fires every eighteen minutes. M. already knew which seconds. Now we need to know how fast.
outro:
Carol marks the six windows on the board. Eighteen minutes between each one. She writes the number in the gap and steps back.

Pre-aggregated metrics don't lie the way logs can. Logs get dropped. Counts are already compressed. Whatever filled this table ran on a schedule for at least three hours.

M. was right about the seconds. Now she's asking what it was trying to do.
